text: Blessed is the man Who walks not in the counsel of the ungodly, Nor stands in the path of sinners, Nor sits in the seat of the scornful;
Why do the nations rage, And the people plot a vain thing?
Lord, how they have increased who trouble me! Many are they who rise up against me.
Hear me when I call, O God of my righteousness! You have relieved me in my distress; Have mercy on me, and hear my prayer.
Give ear to my words, O Lord, Consider my meditation.
O Lord, do not rebuke me in Your anger, Nor chasten me in Your hot displeasure.
O Lord my God, in You I put my trust; Save me from all those who persecute me; And deliver me,
O Lord, our Lord, How excellent is Your name in all the earth, Who have set Your glory above the heavens!
I will praise You, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will tell of all Your marvelous works.
Why do You stand afar off, O Lord? Why do You hide in times of trouble?
In the Lord I put my trust; How can you say to my soul, "Flee as a bird to your mountain"?
Help, Lord, for the godly man ceases! For the faithful disappear from among the sons of men.
How long, O Lord? Will You forget me forever? How long will You hide Your face from me?
The fool has said in his heart, "There is no God." They are corrupt, They have done abominable works, There is none who does good.
Lord, who may abide in Your tabernacle? Who may dwell in Your holy hill?
Preserve me, O God, for in You I put my trust.
Hear a just cause, O Lord, Attend to my cry; Give ear to my prayer which is not from deceitful lips.
I will love You, O Lord, my strength.
The heavens declare the glory of God; And the firmament shows His handiwork.
May the Lord answer you in the day of trouble; May the name of the God of Jacob defend you;
The king shall have joy in Your strength, O Lord; And in Your salvation how greatly shall he rejoice!
My God, My God, why have You forsaken Me? Why are You so far from helping Me, And from the words of My groaning?
The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.
The earth is the Lord’s, and all its fullness, The world and those who dwell therein.
To You, O Lord, I lift up my soul.
Vindicate me, O Lord, For I have walked in my integrity. I have also trusted in the Lord; I shall not slip.
The Lord is my light and my salvation; Whom shall I fear? The Lord is the strength of my life; Of whom shall I be afraid?
To You I will cry, O Lord my rock: Do not be silent to me, Lest, if You are silent to me, I become like those who go down into the pit.
Give unto the Lord, O you mighty ones, Give unto the Lord glory and strength.
I will extol You, O Lord, for You have lifted me up, And have not let my foes rejoice over me.
In You, O Lord, I put my trust; Let me never be ashamed; Deliver me in Your righteousness.
Blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven, Whose sin is covered.
Rejoice in the Lord, O you righteous! For praise from the upright is beautiful.
I will bless the Lord at all times; His praise shall continually be in my mouth.
Plead my cause, O Lord, with those who strive with me; Fight against those who fight against me.
An oracle within my heart concerning the transgression of the wicked: There is no fear of God before his eyes.
Do not fret because of evildoers, Nor be envious of the workers of iniquity.
O Lord, do not rebuke me in Your wrath, Nor chasten me in Your hot displeasure.
I said, "I will guard my ways, Lest I sin with my tongue; I will restrain my mouth with a muzzle, While the wicked are before me."
I waited patiently for the Lord; And He inclined to me, And heard my cry.
Blessed is he who considers the poor; The Lord will deliver him in time of trouble.
As the deer pants for the water brooks, So pants my soul for You, O God.
Vindicate me, O God, And plead my cause against an ungodly nation; Oh, deliver me from the deceitful and unjust man!
We have heard with our ears, O God, Our fathers have told us, The deeds You did in their days, In days of old:
My heart is overflowing with a good theme; I recite my composition concerning the King; My tongue is the pen of a ready writer.
God is our refuge and strength, A very present help in trouble.
Oh, clap your hands, all you peoples! Shout to God with the voice of triumph!
Great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised In the city of our God, In His holy mountain.
Hear this, all you peoples; Give ear, all you inhabitants of the world,
The Mighty One, God the Lord, Has spoken and called the earth From the rising of the sun to its going down.
Have mercy upon me, O God, According to Your lovingkindness; According to the multitude of Your tender mercies, Blot out my transgressions.
Why do you boast in mischief, O mighty man? The goodness of God endures continually.
The fool has said in his heart, "There is no God." They are corrupt, and have done abominable iniquity; There is none who does good.
Save me, O God, by Your name, And vindicate me by Your strength.
Give ear to my prayer, O God, And do not hide Yourself from my supplication.
Be merciful to me, O God, for man would swallow me up; Fighting all day he oppresses me.
Be merciful to me, O God, be merciful to me! For my soul trusts in You; And in the shadow of Your wings I will make my refuge, Until these calamities have passed by.
Do you indeed speak righteousness, you silent ones? Do you judge uprightly, you sons of men?
Deliver me from my enemies, O my God; Defend me from those who rise up against me.
O God, You have cast us off, You have broken us; You have been displeased; Oh, restore us again!
Hear my cry, O God; Attend to my prayer.
Truly my soul silently waits for God; From Him comes my salvation.
O God, You are my God; Early will I seek You; My soul thirsts for You; My flesh longs for You In a dry and thirsty land Where there is no water.
Hear my voice, O God, in my meditation; Preserve my life from fear of the enemy.
Praise is awaiting You, O God, in Zion; And to You the vow shall be performed.
Make a joyful shout to God, all the earth!
God be merciful to us and bless us, And cause His face to shine upon us, Selah
Let God arise, Let His enemies be scattered; Let those also who hate Him flee before Him.
Save me, O God! For the waters have come up to my neck.
Make haste, O God, to deliver me! Make haste to help me, O Lord!
In You, O Lord, I put my trust; Let me never be put to shame.
Give the king Your judgments, O God, And Your righteousness to the king’s Son.
Truly God is good to Israel, To such as are pure in heart.
O God, why have You cast us off forever? Why does Your anger smoke against the sheep of Your pasture?
We give thanks to You, O God, we give thanks! For Your wondrous works declare that Your name is near.
In Judah God is known; His name is great in Israel.
I cried out to God with my voice— To God with my voice; And He gave ear to me.
Give ear, O my people, to my law; Incline your ears to the words of my mouth.
O God, the nations have come into Your inheritance; Your holy temple they have defiled; They have laid Jerusalem in heaps.
Give ear, O Shepherd of Israel, You who lead Joseph like a flock; You who dwell between the cherubim, shine forth!
Sing aloud to God our strength; Make a joyful shout to the God of Jacob.
God stands in the congregation of the mighty; He judges among the gods.
Do not keep silent, O God! Do not hold Your peace, And do not be still, O God!
How lovely is Your dwelling place, O Lord of hosts!
Lord, You have been favorable to Your land; You have brought back the captivity of Jacob.
Bow down Your ear, O Lord, hear me; For I am poor and needy.
His foundation is in the holy mountains.
O Lord, God of my salvation, I have cried out day and night before You.
I will sing of the mercies of the Lord forever; With my mouth will I make known Your faithfulness to all generations.
Lord, You have been our dwelling place in all generations.
He who dwells in the secret place of the Most High Shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.
It is good to give thanks to the Lord, And to sing praises to Your name, O Most High;
The Lord reigns, He is clothed with majesty; The Lord is clothed, He has girded Himself with strength. Surely the world is established, so that it cannot be moved.
O Lord God, to whom vengeance belongs— O God, to whom vengeance belongs, shine forth!
Oh come, let us sing to the Lord! Let us shout joyfully to the Rock of our salvation.
Oh, sing to the Lord a new song! Sing to the Lord, all the earth.
The Lord reigns; Let the earth rejoice; Let the multitude of isles be glad!
Oh, sing to the Lord a new song! For He has done marvelous things; His right hand and His holy arm have gained Him the victory.
The Lord reigns; Let the peoples tremble! He dwells between the cherubim; Let the earth be moved!
Make a joyful shout to the Lord, all you lands!
I will sing of mercy and justice; To You, O Lord, I will sing praises.
Hear my prayer, O Lord, And let my cry come to You.
Bless the Lord, O my soul; And all that is within me, bless His holy name!
Bless the Lord, O my soul! O Lord my God, You are very great: You are clothed with honor and majesty,
Oh, give thanks to the Lord! Call upon His name; Make known His deeds among the peoples!
Praise the Lord! Oh, give thanks to the Lord, for He is good! For His mercy endures forever.
Oh, give thanks to the Lord, for He is good! For His mercy endures forever.
O God, my heart is steadfast; I will sing and give praise, even with my glory.
Do not keep silent, O God of my praise!
The Lord said to my Lord, "Sit at My right hand, Till I make Your enemies Your footstool."
Praise the Lord! I will praise the Lord with my whole heart, In the assembly of the upright and in the congregation.
Praise the Lord! Blessed is the man who fears the Lord, Who delights greatly in His commandments.
Praise the Lord! Praise, O servants of the Lord, Praise the name of the Lord!
When Israel went out of Egypt, The house of Jacob from a people of strange language,
Not unto us, O Lord, not unto us, But to Your name give glory, Because of Your lovingkindness, Because of Your truth.
I love the Lord, because He has heard My voice and my supplications.
Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles! Laud Him, all you peoples!
Oh, give thanks to the Lord, for He is good! Because His mercy endures forever.
Blessed are the undefiled in the way, Who walk in the law of the Lord!
In my distress I cried to the Lord, And He heard me.
I will lift up my eyes to the hills— From whence comes my help?
I was glad when they said to me, "Let us go into the house of the Lord."
Unto You I lift up my eyes, O You who dwell in the heavens.
If it had not been the Lord who was on our side, Let Israel now say—
Those who trust in the Lord Are like Mount Zion, Which cannot be moved, but abides forever.
When the Lord brought back the captivity of Zion, We were like those who dream.
Unless the Lord builds the house, They labor in vain who build it; Unless the Lord guards the city, The watchman stays awake in vain.
Blessed is every one who fears the Lord, Who walks in His ways.
Many a time they have afflicted me from my youth, Let Israel now say—
Out of the depths I have cried to You, O Lord;
Lord, my heart is not haughty, Nor my eyes lofty. Neither do I concern myself with great matters, Nor with things too profound for me.
Lord, remember David And all his afflictions;
Behold, how good and how pleasant it is For brethren to dwell together in unity!
Behold, bless the Lord, All you servants of the Lord, Who by night stand in the house of the Lord!
Praise the Lord! Praise the name of the Lord; Praise Him, O you servants of the Lord!
Oh, give thanks to the Lord, for He is good! For His mercy endures forever.
By the rivers of Babylon, There we sat down, yea, we wept When we remembered Zion.
I will praise You with my whole heart; Before the gods I will sing praises to You.
O Lord, You have searched me and known me.
Deliver me, O Lord, from evil men; Preserve me from violent men,
Lord, I cry out to You; Make haste to me! Give ear to my voice when I cry out to You.
I cry out to the Lord with my voice; With my voice to the Lord I make my supplication.
Hear my prayer, O Lord, Give ear to my supplications! In Your faithfulness answer me, And in Your righteousness.
Blessed be the Lord my Rock, Who trains my hands for war, And my fingers for battle—
I will extol You, my God, O King; And I will bless Your name forever and ever.
Praise the Lord! Praise the Lord, O my soul!
Praise the Lord! For it is good to sing praises to our God; For it is pleasant, and praise is beautiful.
Praise the Lord! Praise the Lord from the heavens; Praise Him in the heights!
Praise the Lord! Sing to the Lord a new song, And His praise in the congregation of saints.
Praise the Lord! Praise God in His sanctuary; Praise Him in His mighty firmament!
